common.failedToLoad: Failed to load. Please refresh the page.

landing.subtitle: Choose Council for answers. Choose Advisors for decisions.

council.label: LLM Council
council.title: Multi-Model Deliberation
council.desc: Multiple AI models answer in parallel, rank each other anonymously, and a chairman synthesizes the best response.
council.feature1: Best for facts, summaries, creative prompts
council.feature2: Use when you want one strong answer

advisors.label: LLM Advisors
advisors.title: Persona-Driven Debate
advisors.desc: Named advisor personas argue through choices where the right answer depends on priorities, risks, and tradeoffs.
advisors.feature1: Best for strategy, ethics, risk, prioritization
advisors.feature2: Use when reasonable advisors may disagree

general.uiLanguageDescription: The language used for all UI text (menus, buttons, settings). This does not affect the language models reply in - that is configured separately below.

chat.welcomeMessage: Configure your council below, then start a session or ask your question.
chat.welcomeMessageShort: Configure your council below, then ask your question.
chat.councilNotReady: Council not ready - add at least one member.

executionMode.needsChairman: Select a chairman model to enable Full Deliberation

stage2.title: Stage 2: Peer Rankings
stage2.rawEvalDescription: Each model evaluated all responses (anonymized as {{labels}}) and provided rankings. Below, model names are shown in bold for readability, but the original evaluation used anonymous labels.
stage2.leaderboardDescription: Combined results across all peer evaluations. Bar length corresponds to average rank value.
stage2.matrixDescription: Detailed matrix of anonymous peer evaluations.
stage2.extractedRankingTooltip: This is the ranking parsed from the model's text response. It's used to calculate the aggregate rankings below. Compare with the text above to verify the system correctly understood the model's ranking.

stage4.title: Stage 4: Corrected Draft
stage4.description: The chairman has rewritten the original document incorporating all corrections, fixing flawed claims, and applying recommendations from the deliberation. Changes are marked with [REVISED] or [NEW].

costReport.totalTokensTooltip: Provider-reported total tokens when available, otherwise input plus output tokens.

councilSetup.newCouncilClearTitle: Clear all current members and chairman to start fresh
councilSetup.overwriteWarning: Saving will replace "{{name}}" with your current lineup.

settingsShell.title: Settings
settingsShell.defaultsLoadedMsg: Defaults loaded. Please configure an API Key.

backup.heading: Backup & Reset
backup.description: Save or restore your council configuration (models, prompts, settings).
backup.noteApiKeys: Note: API keys are NOT exported for security.
backup.dangerDesc: Reset all settings to their default values. This will clear your council selection and custom prompts. API keys will be preserved.
backup.confirmPrompt: Are you sure you want to reset to defaults?

generalExtra.heading: General
generalExtra.description: Display and language preferences for the application interface and model responses. Changes save automatically.
generalExtra.responseLanguageDescription: Council and advisor models will be instructed to respond in this language. Conversation titles and internal search queries stay in English.

providers.heading: LLM API Keys
providers.description: Configure keys for LLM providers. Keys are auto-saved immediately upon successful test.
providers.opencodeDescription: One OpenCode API key unlocks Zen (curated, per-token) and Go (subscription). v1 supports OpenAI-compatible chat/completions models only.
providers.customDescription: Connect to any OpenAI-compatible API (Together AI, Fireworks, vLLM, LM Studio, etc.)
providers.displayNamePlaceholder: e.g., Together AI, My vLLM Server

councilConfig.sourcesDescription: Toggle which providers are available across all model pickers - Council members, Chairman, and Advisor debates. Disabling a provider here hides its models everywhere.
councilConfig.sourcesNote: Note: Most non-chat models (embeddings, image generation, speech, OCR, etc.) are automatically filtered out, though some may still appear.
councilConfig.tempDescription: Temperature controls how creative or focused a model's responses are. Lower values produce more predictable, consistent outputs; higher values encourage more diverse, creative answers.
councilConfig.fixedTempWarning: Some selected models enforce fixed temperature and will ignore this setting.
councilConfig.fixedTempWarningChairman: This model enforces fixed temperature and will ignore this setting.
councilConfig.tooltipOllama: Not configured - connect Ollama in LLM API Keys
councilConfig.tooltipApiKey: Not configured - add API key in LLM API Keys
councilConfig.tooltipSourceMissing: SOURCE NOT CONFIGURED - Add API key in LLM API Keys

debate.heading: Council Debate Config
debate.description: Configure the multi-round iterative debate pipeline, convergence parameters, and critique formatting for the Council deliberation.
debate.note: Note: These settings apply strictly to the main multi-model Council and do not affect the Advisor debate setups.
debate.freeformR1Desc: Each model answers your question independently - no awareness of the others.
debate.freeformR2Desc: Each model receives the Chairman's synthesis from the previous round plus their ranking position, then rewrites their response based on that collective output.
debate.freeformBestDesc: Most questions. Fastest execution, no extra API calls, and the quality improvement per round is already significant.
debate.paragraphR1Desc: Same as Free-form, but every response is auto-numbered by paragraph before peer review. Reviewers must cite paragraph numbers in their critiques.
debate.paragraphR2Desc: Each model is shown exactly which of their own paragraphs peers called strong or weak, plus up to 5 top-rated paragraphs from rival models to potentially incorporate.
debate.paragraphBestDesc: Structured essays, multi-part answers, legal/policy analysis - any response that naturally breaks into distinct sections worth critiquing individually.
debate.claimBeforeDesc: The Chairman runs an extra API call to extract factual claims from all responses into structured JSON. Reviewers verdict each claim as strong, weak, or neutral - not the whole response.
debate.claimR2Desc: Each model sees which of their own claims peers rejected, and the top claims from rivals that got strong agreement - so rewrites are targeted at specific disputed facts, not general impressions.
debate.claimBestDesc: Fact-heavy queries, technical auditing, or any topic where you care about specific factual accuracy rather than overall argument quality. Adds 1 extra API call per round.
debate.meterSingleDesc: Standard one-pass response. Fastest execution, zero debate overhead, and minimal token cost.
debate.meterBalancedDesc: Highly Recommended. Models engage in peer review & critique, driving optimal analytical depth without excessive token costs.
debate.meterDeepDesc: Maximum convergence depth. Warning: Consumes significantly higher API tokens and introduces noticeable latency per model.
debate.autoConvergeHint: Stop early if peer rankings stabilize
debate.thresholdHint: Early stopping triggers if the relative rankings of all response drafts remain identical for {{n}} consecutive rounds. Saves significant API tokens!
debate.warnNotActive: Multi-round debate is configured but currently not active. Change Execution Mode to
debate.warnNotActive3: in the main screen to enable it.
debate.warnChatOnly2: . Deliberative peer review stages and synthetic convergence are bypassed.

prompts.councilDescription: Customize the system prompts used by the council workflow.
prompts.advisorDescription: Customize the orchestration prompts used by advisor debates.

council.stage1Description: Used when each council model answers the user question independently.
council.stage2Description: Used when council models anonymously review and rank the Stage 1 responses.
council.stage3Description: Used when the chairman model synthesizes the council output into the final answer.
council.stage4Description: Used when the chairman synthesizes claims and edits the previous responses into a finalized, revised draft after multiple debate rounds.
council.titleDescription: Used to generate a short title for saved conversations.
council.queryDescription: Used to turn a user question into a concise web search query.

advisor.round1Description: Used for each advisor opening statement before the debate has peer claims to rebut.
advisor.followupDescription: Used after Round 1 so advisors respond to the distilled strongest claims from peers.
advisor.extractDescription: Used by the tiebreaker model after each round to summarize the strongest claims.
advisor.verdictDescription: Used to synthesize the full advisor debate and the debate arc into a final verdict.
advisor.tiebreakerDescription: Used when two advisors fail to reach consensus and a tiebreaker verdict is needed.

claimCards.description: Claims extracted from each response and evaluated by all peers.
claimCards.noContested: No claims were contested in any round. Full consensus across all evaluators.

rankingHeatmap.title: 📊 Peer Deliberation Matrix
rankingHeatmap.description: Detailed matrix of anonymous peer reviews. Raters are on the left; rated responses are on top. Self-review cells (-) are excluded from the grid but counted as a perfect 1st place (1.00) in aggregate score calculations to match the leaderboard averages.

advisorSetup.questionPlaceholder: What should your advisors debate? (Shift+Enter for new line)
advisorSetup.name: Name
advisorSetup.enterPrompt: ↵ Enter to start · Shift+Enter for new line
advisorSetup.fillForm: Fill in the form below, then start your debate

search.fullArticleDesc: Uses Jina AI to read the full text of the top search results.
search.ddgDesc: DuckDuckGo includes built-in intelligent query processing that automatically:
search.ddgFeature1: Removes conversational fluff from your prompts
search.ddgFeature3: Adds temporal context for current events
search.resultCountHint: More results = better coverage but slower. Default: 8
search.hybridSearchHint: Combines general web results with recent news for better coverage of current events.
search.queryProcessingDesc: Choose how your prompt is sent to the search engine.
search.ddgInfoNote: ℹ️ DuckDuckGo uses built-in query optimization. Direct mode is recommended.
search.directDesc: Send your exact query to the search engine. Best for most providers.
search.smartKeywordsDesc: Extract key terms from your prompt before searching. Useful if you paste very long prompts that confuse the search engine.
search.llmReformulationDesc: Use the Chairman model to rephrase your query into an optimal search term. Slower but can improve results for complex questions.
